    <h3 {...{
      "id": "common-sources-of-confusion"
    }}>{`Common Sources of Confusion`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"I need to access the document"`}</strong>{` - Which document?`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Adobe Express user's project → "The document has 3 pages"`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Manipulate Adobe Express content → `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`editor.documentRoot`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Import/export Adobe Express document → `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.app.document`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Your add-on's UI HTML page → `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`document.getElementById()`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"What's the DOM?"`}</strong>{` - Which DOM?`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Your add-on's HTML structure → `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`document.querySelector()`}</inlineCode>{` (Browser DOM)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Adobe Express's document structure → Use "scenegraph" not "DOM"`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"How do I use the runtime?"`}</strong>{` - Which runtime?`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Execution environment → "Code runs in iframe runtime or document sandbox"`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Communication APIs → `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk.instance.runtime`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"What is the context?"`}</strong>{` - Which context?`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Execution environment → "The iframe context has standard Web APIs"`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Editor's selection/insertion → `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`editor.context.selection`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Security boundaries → See `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../platform_concepts/context.md"
        }}>{`iframe Context & Security`}</a></li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"What does instance mean?"`}</strong>{` - Which instance?`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`SDK property → `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.instance`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnSandboxSdk.instance`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Class object → "rectangle is an instance of RectangleNode"`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Running session → "User's add-on instance"`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"How do I import SDKs?"`}</strong>{` - Named or default export?`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Add-on UI SDK → Default export (no curly braces): `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`import addOnUISdk from "..."`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Document Sandbox SDK → Default export (no curly braces): `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`import addOnSandboxSdk from "..."`}</inlineCode></li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Express Document SDK → Named exports (requires curly braces): `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`import { editor, colorUtils } from "..."`}</inlineCode></li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"Are Web APIs and Browser APIs the same?"`}</strong>{` - Yes!`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Same thing, different names → "Web APIs" = "Browser APIs"`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`iframe runtime → Standard Web APIs available`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Document sandbox → Limited Web APIs only (console, Blob)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"Should I say node or element?"`}</strong>{` - It depends!`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Adobe Express scenegraph → Use "node" (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`RectangleNode`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`TextNode`}</inlineCode>{`, etc.) to describe visual elements in the document`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Your add-on's HTML → Use "DOM node" or "HTML element"`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`User-facing docs → "element" is okay (e.g., "text elements in your design")`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Developer docs → Prefer "node" for precision and to match API class names`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`"Should I use `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`addOnUISdk.instance`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="strong">{`addOnUISdk.app`}</inlineCode>{`?"`}</strong>{` - Different scopes`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Add-on scope`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`instance`}</inlineCode>{`) → Features specific to YOUR add-on`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{` - YOUR add-on's communication`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`instance.clientStorage`}</inlineCode>{` - YOUR add-on's storage (per-user, per-addon)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`instance.manifest`}</inlineCode>{` - YOUR add-on's configuration`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Application scope`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`app`}</inlineCode>{`) → Features shared across Adobe Express`}
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.document`}</inlineCode>{` - The Adobe Express document (same for all add-ons)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.currentUser`}</inlineCode>{` - The Express user (not specific to your add-on)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.ui`}</inlineCode>{` - Adobe Express UI state (`}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`theme`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`locale`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
    </ul>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "runtime-environments"
    }}>{`Runtime Environments`}</h2>
    <p>{`For security reasons, Adobe Express add-ons use a `}<strong parentName="p">{`dual-runtime architecture`}</strong>{` with two separate JavaScript execution environments:`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "iframe-runtime"
    }}><strong parentName="h3">{`Iframe Runtime`}</strong></h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`What it is`}</strong>{`: A sandboxed iframe environment where your add-on's user interface runs`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Purpose`}</strong>{`: Hosts your HTML, CSS, and JavaScript UI code`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`SDK Used`}</strong>{`: Add-on UI SDK`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`File reference`}</strong>{`: Typically your `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`index.html`}</inlineCode>{` and associated UI JavaScript files`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Security`}</strong>{`: Sandboxed for security with standard Web APIs (some features require `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "../../../references/manifest/index.md"
        }}>{`manifest permissions`}</a>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Also known as`}</strong>{`: "Panel Runtime", "iframe Sandbox"`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Terminology Note`}</strong>{`: While the browser term is "iframe sandbox" (as used in `}<a parentName="li" {...{
          "href": "https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTML/Element/iframe#sandbox"
        }}>{`HTML sandbox attributes`}</a>{` and manifest permissions), we use "iframe runtime" throughout the documentation for consistency with "document sandbox runtime" and to distinguish between the two execution environments`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "document-sandbox"
    }}><strong parentName="h3">{`Document Sandbox`}</strong></h3>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`What it is`}</strong>{`: A separate sandboxed JavaScript environment for document manipulation`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Purpose`}</strong>{`: Provides secure access to Adobe Express document structure and content`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`SDKs Used`}</strong>{`: Document Sandbox SDK (for communication) + Express Document SDK (for document APIs)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`File reference`}</strong>{`: Specified in your manifest's `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`documentSandbox`}</inlineCode>{` entry (e.g., `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`code.js`}</inlineCode>{`)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Security`}</strong>{`: Isolated environment with limited Web APIs but direct document access`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Also known as`}</strong>{`: "Document Model Sandbox"`}</li>
    </ul>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "understanding-runtime-communication"
    }}>{`Understanding Runtime Communication`}</h4>
    <p>{`The two runtimes communicate with each other through the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/communication/index.md"
      }}>{`Communication APIs`}</a>{`, allowing your UI to trigger document changes and vice versa.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`About Web APIs`}</strong>{`: The terms "browser capabilities," "browser features," and "Web APIs" all refer to the standard JavaScript APIs available in web environments (like `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fetch`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`localStorage`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`console`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Blob`}</inlineCode>{`, etc.). The iframe runtime has standard Web APIs, while the document sandbox has limited Web APIs for security reasons. See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../../references/document-sandbox/web/index.md"
      }}>{`Web APIs Reference`}</a>{` for details on what's available in each environment.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p>{`For a comprehensive deep-dive into the dual-runtime architecture with visual diagrams, communication patterns, and code examples, see the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../platform_concepts/architecture.md"
      }}>{`Add-on Architecture Guide`}</a>{` and the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../../learn/how_to/tutorials/stats-addon.md"
      }}>{`Communication APIs Tutorial`}</a>{`.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "sdk-concepts"
    }}>{`SDK Concepts`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "add-on-ui-sdk"
    }}>{`Add-on UI SDK`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`addOnUISdk`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: The JavaScript module you import in your iframe code that provides `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`runtime`}</inlineCode>{` instance, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`app`}</inlineCode>{` interfaces, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`constants`}</inlineCode>{`, and UI-specific APIs.`}</p>
    <p>{`The `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addOnUISdk`}</inlineCode>{` object provides two distinct scopes of functionality:`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`addOnUISdk.instance`}</inlineCode>{` - Add-on Scope`}</strong><br parentName="p"></br>{`
`}{`Features specific to your individual add-on:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`runtime`}</inlineCode>{` - Communication between your add-on's iframe and document sandbox`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`clientStorage`}</inlineCode>{` - Data storage for your add-on only (per-user, per-addon)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`manifest`}</inlineCode>{` - Your add-on's configuration`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`entrypointType`}</inlineCode>{` - Your add-on's current entry point`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`logger`}</inlineCode>{` - Logging for your add-on`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Scope`}</strong>{`: Isolated to your add-on instance; doesn't interact with other add-ons.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`addOnUISdk.app`}</inlineCode>{` - Application Scope`}</strong><br parentName="p"></br>{`
`}{`Features shared across Adobe Express (the host application):`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`document`}</inlineCode>{` - The active Adobe Express document (shared across all add-ons)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`oauth`}</inlineCode>{` - Authentication with external services`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`currentUser`}</inlineCode>{` - The Adobe Express user (not specific to your add-on)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`ui`}</inlineCode>{` - Adobe Express UI state (theme, locale, etc.)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`command`}</inlineCode>{` - Commands in the host application`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Scope`}</strong>{`: Interacts with Adobe Express itself and its global state.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "express-document-sdk"
    }}>{`Express Document SDK`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Express Document SDK`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`express-document-sdk`}</inlineCode>{`): The JavaScript module providing document manipulation capabilities for the document sandbox. Import named exports: `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`editor`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`colorUtils`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`constants`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fonts`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`viewport`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "document-sandbox-sdk"
    }}>{`Document Sandbox SDK`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Document Sandbox SDK`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`add-on-sdk-document-sandbox`}</inlineCode>{`): The JavaScript module for communication between iframe runtime and document sandbox. Import as `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addOnSandboxSdk`}</inlineCode>{`. Only needed when you require bi-directional communication between the two environments.`}</p>
    <InlineAlert variant="info" slots="text" mdxType="InlineAlert" />
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Note`}</strong>{`: All Adobe Express add-on SDKs use the singleton pattern - pre-instantiated objects you import and use directly. You never create new instances yourself. See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "#q-what-is-the-singleton-pattern-and-why-do-add-on-sdks-use-it"
      }}>{`FAQ on singleton pattern`}</a>{` and `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../platform_concepts/architecture.md#sdk-structure--import-patterns"
      }}>{`Architecture Guide`}</a>{` for details.`}</p>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "import-patterns--usage"

        "className": "language-js"

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "communication-system"
    }}>{`Communication System`}</h2>
    <h3 {...{
      "id": "bidirectional-communication-pattern"
    }}>{`Bidirectional Communication Pattern`}</h3>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`iframe runtime → document sandbox`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// iframe runtime (index.js)
const { runtime } = addOnUISdk.instance;
const sandboxProxy = await runtime.apiProxy("documentSandbox");
await sandboxProxy.createRectangle();

// document sandbox (code.js)
const { runtime } = addOnSandboxSdk.instance;  // Document Sandbox SDK
runtime.exposeApi({
    createRectangle() {
        const rect = editor.createRectangle();
        editor.context.insertionParent.children.append(rect);
    }
});
`}</code></pre>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`document sandbox → iframe runtime`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// document sandbox (code.js)
const panelProxy = await runtime.apiProxy("panel");
await panelProxy.updateUI("Rectangle created");

// iframe runtime (index.js)
runtime.exposeApi({
    updateUI(message) {
        document.getElementById('status').textContent = message;
    }
});
`}</code></pre>

      "id": "runtime-types"
    }}>{`Runtime Types`}</h3>

      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`panel`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: The main iframe runtime for your add-on UI`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`documentSandbox`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: The document manipulation runtime`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`dialog`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Runtime context when code is running within a modal dialog`}</li>
    </ul>

    <h2 {...{
      "id": "node-hierarchy"
    }}>{`Node Hierarchy`}</h2>
    <p>{`Adobe Express documents are structured as a `}<strong parentName="p">{`scenegraph`}</strong>{` - a hierarchical tree of nodes representing visual elements.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`BaseNode`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: The minimal base class for all document elements with basic properties like `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`id`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`type`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`parent`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`allChildren`}</inlineCode>{`.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`Node`}</inlineCode></strong>{`: Full-featured visual content that extends `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`BaseNode`}</inlineCode>{` with visual properties and transformations.`}</p>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`Common Node Types`}</strong>{`:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Container Nodes`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ArtboardNode`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`GroupNode`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`PageNode`}</inlineCode>{` (hold other elements)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Content Nodes`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`RectangleNode`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`EllipseNode`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`TextNode`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`LineNode`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`PathNode`}</inlineCode>{` (visual elements)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><strong parentName="li">{`Media Nodes`}</strong>{`: `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`MediaContainerNode`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`ImageRectangleNode`}</inlineCode>{` (images and media)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <pre><code parentName="pre" {...{
        "className": "language-js"
      }}>{`// Navigate the document hierarchy
const root = editor.documentRoot;           // ExpressRootNode
const currentPage = root.pages.first;       // PageNode
const artboard = currentPage.artboards.first; // ArtboardNode

// Create and add content
const rectangle = editor.createRectangle(); // RectangleNode
artboard.children.append(rectangle);
`}</code></pre>

    <hr></hr>
    <h2 {...{
      "id": "faqs"
    }}>{`FAQs`}</h2>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-whats-the-difference-between-add-on-ui-sdk-and-document-apis"
    }}>{`Q: What's the difference between "Add-on UI SDK" and "Document APIs"?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` The `}<strong parentName="p">{`Add-on UI SDK`}</strong>{` runs in the iframe runtime and handles UI, user interactions, and import/export. `}<strong parentName="p">{`Document APIs`}</strong>{` (via Express Document SDK) run in the document sandbox and handle content creation and document manipulation.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-why-are-there-two-different-runtime-environments"
    }}>{`Q: Why are there two different runtime environments?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Security and performance. The `}<strong parentName="p">{`iframe runtime`}</strong>{` is sandboxed for security but has standard Web APIs. The `}<strong parentName="p">{`document sandbox`}</strong>{` has direct access to Adobe Express's document engine but limited Web APIs.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-can-i-use-document-apis-directly-from-the-iframe-runtime"
    }}>{`Q: Can I use Document APIs directly from the iframe runtime?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` No, Document APIs (Express Document SDK) are only available in the document sandbox for security reasons. You must use the communication system (Document Sandbox SDK) to bridge between environments.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-when-do-i-use-addonuisdk-vs-addonsandboxsdk"
    }}>{`Q: When do I use `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`addOnUISdk`}</inlineCode>{` vs `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`addOnSandboxSdk`}</inlineCode>{`?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addOnUISdk`}</inlineCode>{` (Add-on UI SDK) in your `}<strong parentName="p">{`iframe runtime code`}</strong>{` (usually `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`index.html`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ui/`}</inlineCode>{` folder). Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`addOnSandboxSdk`}</inlineCode>{` (Document Sandbox SDK) in your `}<strong parentName="p">{`document sandbox code`}</strong>{` (usually `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`code.js`}</inlineCode>{` or `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`sandbox/`}</inlineCode>{` folder).`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-i-see-references-to-ui-sdk---is-this-different-from-add-on-ui-sdk"
    }}>{`Q: I see references to "UI SDK" - is this different from "Add-on UI SDK"?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` No, they're the same. `}<strong parentName="p">{`"Add-on UI SDK"`}</strong>{` is the full, preferred term for clarity, but "UI SDK" is commonly used as shorthand throughout the documentation.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-whats-the-difference-between-addonuisdkinstance-and-addonuisdkapp"
    }}>{`Q: What's the difference between `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`addOnUISdk.instance`}</inlineCode>{` and `}<inlineCode parentName="h4">{`addOnUISdk.app`}</inlineCode>{`?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` These represent `}<strong parentName="p">{`different scopes`}</strong>{` of functionality:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`addOnUISdk.instance`}</inlineCode></strong>{` - `}<strong parentName="p">{`Add-on scope`}</strong>{`: Features specific to YOUR add-on`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`instance.runtime`}</inlineCode>{` - Communication for YOUR add-on`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`instance.clientStorage`}</inlineCode>{` - Storage for YOUR add-on only (per-user, per-addon)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`instance.manifest`}</inlineCode>{` - YOUR add-on's configuration`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
      <li parentName="ul">
        <p parentName="li"><strong parentName="p"><inlineCode parentName="strong">{`addOnUISdk.app`}</inlineCode></strong>{` - `}<strong parentName="p">{`Application scope`}</strong>{`: Features shared across Adobe Express`}</p>
        <ul parentName="li">
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.document`}</inlineCode>{` - The Adobe Express document (same for all add-ons)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.currentUser`}</inlineCode>{` - The Express user (not specific to your add-on)`}</li>
          <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`app.ui`}</inlineCode>{` - Adobe Express UI state (theme, locale)`}</li>
        </ul>
      </li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`Use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`instance`}</inlineCode>{` for add-on-specific features; use `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`app`}</inlineCode>{` to interact with Adobe Express itself.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-im-confused-by-terms-like-document-context-runtime-and-instance---they-seem-to-mean-different-things-in-different-places"
    }}>{`Q: I'm confused by terms like "document", "context", "runtime", and "instance" - they seem to mean different things in different places?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` Yes! Many terms in add-on development are overloaded with multiple meanings. Check the `}<strong parentName="p"><a parentName="strong" {...{
          "href": "#overloaded-terms-clarification"
        }}>{`Overloaded Terms Clarification`}</a></strong>{` table at the top of this page for complete clarification. For example, "document" can mean:`}</p>
    <ul>
      <li parentName="ul">{`The Adobe Express user's project`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`editor.documentRoot`}</inlineCode>{` (scenegraph manipulation)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul"><inlineCode parentName="li">{`addOnUISdk.app.document`}</inlineCode>{` (import/export operations)`}</li>
      <li parentName="ul">{`Browser DOM `}<inlineCode parentName="li">{`document`}</inlineCode>{` object (your add-on's HTML)`}</li>
    </ul>
    <p>{`This table provides all meanings with examples for 17 commonly overloaded terms.`}</p>
    <h4 {...{
      "id": "q-what-is-the-singleton-pattern-and-why-do-add-on-sdks-use-it"
    }}>{`Q: What is the singleton pattern and why do add-on SDKs use it?`}</h4>
    <p><strong parentName="p">{`A:`}</strong>{` All Adobe Express add-on SDKs use the `}<strong parentName="p">{`singleton pattern`}</strong>{` - they provide pre-instantiated objects you import and use directly. You `}<strong parentName="p">{`never create new instances yourself`}</strong>{`.`}</p>
    <p>{`This ensures all your code works with the same SDK instances, preventing conflicts and maintaining consistent state. For Express Document SDK specifically, you import `}<strong parentName="p">{`lowercase names`}</strong>{` (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`editor`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`colorUtils`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`constants`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`fonts`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`viewport`}</inlineCode>{`) which are singleton objects, NOT the uppercase class names (`}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`Editor`}</inlineCode>{`, `}<inlineCode parentName="p">{`ColorUtils`}</inlineCode>{`, etc.).`}</p>
    <p>{`See the `}<a parentName="p" {...{
        "href": "../platform_concepts/architecture.md#sdk-structure--import-patterns"
      }}>{`Architecture Guide`}</a>{` for complete details.`}</p>
